ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kii nsii-amɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.”
translated: The monkey said to the tortoise that he would be the one to eat the ripe palm fruit on the tree, and the tortoise also said that he would be the one to eat the ripe palm fruit on the tree instead of the monkey.

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: Nte̱tɔ ne̱ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ e̱ waa? Mò̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ i kiŋŋi a ɔ ba kake ne̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ maa de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ kpa‑ɔ na saŋ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi‑o.
translated: What is the name of this river? The name of the river is Kiŋŋi and it flows from the forest and comes to the edge of the town and then flows into the sea.

ref: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
text: Amaa Peetroo a be̱e̱ swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱, ma ba aye̱ba ne̱e̱. Mo̱ e̱ ka Wuribware̱ se̱ ke̱yaa fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ nyi ɔnyare̱ amo̱.”
translated: And Peter answered them, "Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins."

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ saŋ ko̱ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ba, na kaye̱ mɔ gye̱ kɛɛ, na kaye̱ po̱pwɛɛ ba. text: Yeesuu a yɔ mmaŋgyii‑ro na e̱maŋ se̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a baa alɔpo̱ mò̱ ase̱ ke̱gya‑rɔ na alɔŋde̱ se̱ bo̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa alɔpo̱‑ɔ de̱ŋ dabo̱rɔ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ dabo̱rɔ amo̱‑ɔ mɔ a kpaare̱.
